findings.message: The documented guard is still not type-safe. `SingleRoomStatus.cleaned` is `string | false | undefined`, so `toEpochMillis(dbStatus.cleaned)` cannot be called without narrowing first; following this literally will either fail TypeScript or rely on unsafe coercion.

The rehearsal trace still says `PrepareDashboard` must call `useRoomStatusData()` directly and that `usePrepareDashboardData()` does not expose it. That contradicts the adopted no-second-listener approach at lines 85 and 250, so the document is internally inconsistent on the prepare-side wiring.

The key-modules note still presents per-row `useRoomStatusData()` as an option for `BookingRow`, even though the document later resolves that per-row subscriptions are the wrong approach. Tighten this to prop-threading only.

The "8th cell or inline cleanliness pill" note still understates the table impact. If the 8th-cell route is chosen, `TableHeader.tsx` and the notes-row `colSpan={7}` in `view/BookingRow.tsx` also need to change.
